name: The White House
subject: This week is a testament to the American spirit
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e7881144b727029cb5c233961f4d7c23b93e7996662c6e583e01ddd7ecae13666d4d32f78355a75394c96f6d44d3ea62e0b) The White House • May 3, 2018
## The Day Ahead
This morning at 11 a.m. ET, President Donald J. Trump will participate in the [National Day of Prayer](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e785fb843a71ee0d76ab82cb7bda2fd475f8c8add96b380dabcb310e7eeb2a795d9247829e65201a34fba13c30ce9f308f7) with a ceremony in the Rose Garden. [Learn more about President Trump’s stand for faith and freedom.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e786decdf96b1936351e214dde0feaaa4bfbf5bcbeb630b07817b4da1a9660227fcac6e4d99a4b278314aa992ddd865bedf) |
---
|

## This week is a testament to the American spirit
**President Trump has proclaimed this week as Small Business Week.** “Small business owners embody the American pioneering spirit and remind us that determination can turn aspiration into achievement,” the President says. There are nearly 30 million small businesses in the United States employing more than 57 million people.
**This Administration has made those businesses a priority.** The benefits from President Trump’s tax cuts are well-established. Equally important is his focus on deregulation: Businesses with 50 or fewer employees face regulatory costs nearly 20 percent higher than the average for all firms. Last fiscal year, Federal agencies issued 67 deregulatory actions to only 3 regulatory actions—a 22:1 ratio.
**As a result, small businesses are booming**. The economy is growing and wages are rising. Unemployment is at a 17-year low, and according to Wells Fargo, small business optimism is at an 11-year high.
[Learn more about how President Trump is prioritizing small businesses. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e780f0c57fc57bc5084c207077436a390a6e6ce012dcbf7a7950b82a967595087f98140fc78b8ccc3487a16acf9c5c922c7)
**More good news** : [Unemployment claims reach an unprecedented low. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e7836dffb6563595eeb0d2b6c5ee626937c526a5cb2ade92f1a5556d78cf7f4d31b8e11db90e43f06f6d8f52208fa05313c)

---
|

## Pompeo: I will make sure America is always respected
**Yesterday, Mike Pompeo took the oath of office as America’s 70th Secretary of State**. President Trump used the occasion to introduce Secretary Pompeo to America’s diplomatic corps. “Mike is a true American patriot. He has devoted many years of his life to defending America,” the President said.
**Secretary Pompeo laid out his vision for America in the world**. “We will employ tough diplomacy when necessary to put the interests of the American people first,” he said. “I will work to ensure their safety, to preserve their rights, and defend their values. And I will make sure America is always a respected and principled leader on the world stage.”
**“They have such respect for him”** at the CIA, where Pompeo previously served as Director, President Trump said. “They may be the only people that are not very happy right now,” the President joked. “But they’ll be happy. They’ll be happy with our Gina, who’s here today.” [Acting CIA Director Gina Haspel](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e78cae144d8abd3c6bc9b5e6aabbb32d9248ffbb5c4264d0ea84ea1bc2db97b80de740e87e0581b3caffa8fd36e174a5154) is President Trump’s nominee to replace Pompeo at the CIA on a full-time basis.
[Watch President Trump and Secretary Pompeo address the State Department](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e78f551ab3d3af141feeea7aa93593b2144e6f15767cc7a333f4d8adc50e7c907bcdf43ce2cb72075f77f940979ab679d92) and read [their full remarks. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=311f9e1fe32a4e78135db7f9a9875b6123e2817d939500b45660f76220aa7302d3a2206783fb0c4e751cba1577717c55baac53ba095ae1a2)

---
|

Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
**President Donald J. Trump delivers remarks at the swearing-in ceremony for Secretary of State Mike Pompeo** | May 2, 2018

name: Jason Yates
subject: Join with tens of thousands of Christians today
body:
| | The National Day of Prayer
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fsubscribe%2Fweeklydevotional%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180503com01&n=1)
| Peter, Today we participate in the National Day of Prayer joining with tens of thousands of Christians across the nation uniting in prayer. We believe that it’s our God-given responsibility to vote – but we also have a God-given mandate to pray. It’s important that we vote in this primary season, but it’s essential that we first pray! My Faith Votes Influencer and President of the National Day of Prayer Ronnie Floyd wrote a great article, “There’s Always A Time for Prayer”, we wanted to share with you today. How should Christians respond to a mass shooting? A terrorist attack? Nuclear threats? Sexual harassment? Racism? Political unrest? Gun violence? These are the questions people have consistently asked me over the past few years. I must confess I’ve asked myself the same questions many times. Seasons of unrest pit us against the wall and demand we respond to what’s going on around us. Some Christians respond by quoting James 2:26, “ _Faith without works is dead,_ ” and calling fellow believers to take up arms, get involved in the public debate and change things for themselves. Others, seeing all the evil and pain around them, quietly determine to make a change in their own lives, families and communities. If you’re wondering if there’s a wrong or right approach, let me offer you Solomon’s wisdom: _“There is an appointed time for everything ... A time to be silent and a time to speak.”_ (Ecclesiastes 3:1,7) But you know what Scripture never sets a time constraint for? Prayer. _“Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you,”_ Paul writes in 1 Thessalonians. There’s always a time for prayer. But our culture doesn’t seem to think so. Nowadays, our culture dismisses prayer as useless, and accuses Christians who turn to prayer, especially after a tragedy, of using it to escape reality or ignore responsibility. But we turn to prayer because that’s where we must start if we want to find healing. Yes, we must confront head-on the issues we’re facing, seek solutions to our many problems and vote for men and women who will work toward unity, but we must acknowledge that, apart from God, all our efforts fall short. No human plan will accomplish more than God’s intervention. **Prayer should always be our first response, not our last choice. Prayer is not inaction, it’s our greatest action.** Imagine what could happen if every Christian and every church in America committed to pray every day for our country, our communities, our families, our schools and our leaders. America would be a very different place. But please understand, with our personal response to God. 2 Chronicles 7:14 is call to God’s people individually and corporately. It says, “If my people who are called by my name humble themselves, and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven and will forgive their sin and heal their land.” Prayer begins with you and your personal response to God. Prayer moves to the church and her response to God. Then, when these things occur, prayer can change a nation. Today millions of Americans will gather in our nation’s capital and throughout all 50 states for the National Day of Prayer. Millions of voices will rise in unified prayer for our nation, asking God to heal and unite us as a people. I hope you and your family will join us, and together pray for a new fresh movement of God in America. \- Ronnie Floyd Peter, spend time today praying for our nation and join My Faith Votes weekly devotional to pray united with other believers for our nation. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fsubscribe%2Fweeklydevotional&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180503com01&n=2) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180503com01&n=3)
---

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: We cannot defeat Trump with the same establishment candidates
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
#

Sisters and Brothers -
If we are going to defeat right-wing Republicans in 2018 and 2020, we need to win in states that Trump won, like Pennsylvania. And, in my view, the only way we win in those states is by supporting progressive candidates who have the guts to defend working-class families - white, black, Latino, Asian American, Native American - and take on the power and greed of the billionaire class.
Here's some very good news. In Pennsylvania there are three strong progressive candidates who can win their primary fights and win the general election. But they need your help.
As you know, Donald Trump represents something we have never seen before in the history of the United States. It is not just that he represents the interests of the wealthy and powerful. It's that we have never had a president with more authoritarian tendencies than him.
The 2018 midterm elections will be long remembered as a pivotal moment in American history because, if we are successful, we can put an end to the disastrous Trump agenda. If we are not, we will have at least two more years of a rapid shift toward authoritarianism, the further normalization of corruption and the continued rise of oligarchy.
But we cannot defeat Trump and the Republican Party with the same playbook, or by supporting the same kind of candidates long favored by the political establishment and financial elite. That is how we got to where we are today.
That is why I am traveling to Pennsylvania later this week to support three strong progressive candidates: John Fetterman for Lieutenant Governor, Greg Edwards for Congress, and Jess King for Congress. Their primaries are on May 15, and they need your help now. So here is the part where I ask:
**[Split a donation between John Fetterman, Greg Edwards and Jess King. This is our chance to elect three great progressives who will help lead the fight against Donald Trump and the dangerous Republican agenda. They need our help to win in Pennsylvania.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1148546/6384009/-2059778510?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDQvMS82ODQwNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDZiNzgzNS1lZjRlLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzg1ZTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YxVNRFsG64Wb9EkTxc6wbiJ9sEFegM2Va-KgdaEt52c=&refcode=em-ngp-180503)**
Our job in 2018 is to take on and defeat Trump's authoritarianism, xenophobia, racism, sexism, homophobia and religious bigotry. But we can only do that if we run candidates who can rally the American people around an agenda that works for them, not the billionaire class.
For more than a decade now, **John Fetterman** has been an outstanding progressive leader in Pennsylvania. From early in his career he has stood with the working families of the state as he advocated for a $15 per hour minimum wage, Medicare for all, marriage equality and ending the failed war on drugs. He has been an outspoken defender of women’s health, immigrant rights, racial justice and LGBTQ equality.
For the past 25 years, **Greg Edwards** has worked tirelessly to organize for justice in health care, education and economic dignity. The powerful grassroots movement behind Greg Edwards is championing Medicare for all, universal pre-k, debt-free college and raising the minimum wage, and he is living his values of unwavering inclusion by building the diverse coalition Democrats need to win in his district.
**Jess King** has seen firsthand how the biggest corporations have hurt workers and squashed competition. In Congress, she will work to pass Medicare for all, rein in Wall Street, break up corporate monopolies, and support the real job creators: small businesses in our local economies.
These are three great candidates you can be proud to support. Please make a donation today. Please help create a progressive Pennsylvania and a progressive America.
**Split a donation between John Fetterman, Greg Edwards and Jess King today:**
**[https://go.berniesanders.com/progressive-pennsylvania](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1148547/6384010/-2059778510?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDQvMS82ODQwNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDZiNzgzNS1lZjRlLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzg1ZTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YxVNRFsG64Wb9EkTxc6wbiJ9sEFegM2Va-KgdaEt52c=&refcode=em-ngp-180503)**
Candidates like John, Greg and Jess will help us stop Trump while ushering in an era of strong progressive change in this country. Thank you for supporting them. They need our help.
In Solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

clean_body: Sisters and Brothers - If we are going to defeat right-wing Republicans in 2018 and 2020, we need to win in states that Trump won, like Pennsylvania. And, in my view, the only way we win in those states is by supporting progressive candidates who have the guts to defend working-class families - white, black, Latino, Asian American, Native American - and take on the power and greed of the billionaire class. Here's some very good news. In Pennsylvania there are three strong progressive candidates who can win their primary fights and win the general election. But they need your help. As you know, Donald Trump represents something we have never seen before in the history of the United States. It is not just that he represents the interests of the wealthy and powerful. It's that we have never had a president with more authoritarian tendencies than him. The 2018 midterm elections will be long remembered as a pivotal moment in American history because, if we are successful, we can put an end to the disastrous Trump agenda. If we are not, we will have at least two more years of a rapid shift toward authoritarianism, the further normalization of corruption and the continued rise of oligarchy. But we cannot defeat Trump and the Republican Party with the same playbook, or by supporting the same kind of candidates long favored by the political establishment and financial elite. That is how we got to where we are today. That is why I am traveling to Pennsylvania later this week to support three strong progressive candidates: John Fetterman for Lieutenant Governor, Greg Edwards for Congress, and Jess King for Congress. Their primaries are on May 15, and they need your help now. So here is the part where I ask: Our job in 2018 is to take on and defeat Trump's authoritarianism, xenophobia, racism, sexism, homophobia and religious bigotry. But we can only do that if we run candidates who can rally the American people around an agenda that works for them, not the billionaire class. For more than a decade now, John Fetterman has been an outstanding progressive leader in Pennsylvania. From early in his career he has stood with the working families of the state as he advocated for a $15 per hour minimum wage, Medicare for all, marriage equality and ending the failed war on drugs. He has been an outspoken defender of women’s health, immigrant rights, racial justice and LGBTQ equality. For the past 25 years, Greg Edwards has worked tirelessly to organize for justice in health care, education and economic dignity. The powerful grassroots movement behind Greg Edwards is championing Medicare for all, universal pre-k, debt-free college and raising the minimum wage, and he is living his values of unwavering inclusion by building the diverse coalition Democrats need to win in his district. Jess King has seen firsthand how the biggest corporations have hurt workers and squashed competition. In Congress, she will work to pass Medicare for all, rein in Wall Street, break up corporate monopolies, and support the real job creators: small businesses in our local economies. These are three great candidates you can be proud to support. Please make a donation today. Please help create a progressive Pennsylvania and a progressive America. Split a donation between John Fetterman, Greg Edwards and Jess King today: Candidates like John, Greg and Jess will help us stop Trump while ushering in an era of strong progressive change in this country. Thank you for supporting them. They need our help. name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Ok, here are my answers:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
I'm in the RV again and ready to answer your questions! 'll be answering more questions when I go live on Facebook today (May 3) at 11:30 a.m. |
---

| | Peter,

I'm in the RV again and ready to answer your questions! First, I want to thank every single person who wrote in with questions -- I deeply appreciate your engagement.

I'll be answering more questions when I go live on Facebook today (May 3) at 11:30 a.m. If you haven't already, **[please follow me on Facebook.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20bb/7377f838/3ee36c27/51f9422f/2807832297/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/JoeForIndiana/")** Once you click follow, you can select "see first" to make sure we don't miss each other.

I appreciate every one of your questions, and while I don't have the space here to answer all of them, these are the themes that came up the most:

**Charles in Greenfield asked:_What's your stand on privatizing/voucherizing Social Security/Medicare?_**

My answer can be summed up in two words: no way.

Social Security and Medicare are a contract we make with hard-working Hoosiers throughout their lives. We tell them, you work as hard as you can, play by the rules, and pay into the system, and we'll make sure you've got the security you need when you can't work anymore. These programs are fundamental to who we are -- a country that takes care of its people.

Plans to privatize Social Security and Medicare are a scam to cheat people out of their own money that they've paid into the system for their entire working lives and are expecting to be there for them when they retire. It's their money -- not another bank account for Mitch McConnell's allies. That's why I've proudly opposed any privatization plan, and you can bet I'll stay the course if Hoosiers send me back to the Senate in November.

**Chris in Kokomo asked:_Joe, what can be done about_ Citizens United _? In my opinion this is destroying our state and our country._**

I agree that _Citizens United_ is a danger to our democracy. This 2010 Supreme Court decision has opened the floodgates for unlimited anonymous money in politics. I should know -- the Koch brothers and their allies have already spent more than $8 million to defeat me this cycle.

That's why I support a constitutional amendment to overturn _Citizens United_. And to answer your question, Chris, I think the single best thing we can do to fight back is to elect representatives who will push for such an amendment in Washington.

**And finally, an anonymous Hoosier asked:_Why did you vote against the tax cuts?_**

Thanks for asking. I voted against the McConnell-Ryan tax law because it could cost as much as $2.2 trillion over a decade, does far too little for the middle class, and has given most of the benefits to big banks and already extremely profitable corporations.

I tried to work with senators on both sides of the aisle to produce real tax reform that provided tax relief for working families. But that's not what this law does.

This law doesn't deliver relief to hardworking Hoosier families who go to work in the dark and come home in the dark. And any gains they do find are likely to be wiped out by skyrocketing premiums charged by the same insurance companies that are making billions in additional profits thanks to new tax breaks. Recent non-partisan reports said Hoosiers' premiums are likely to rise by more than a third next year -- largely because of changes in the McConnell tax plan that will cause up to 13 million Americans to go without health care within a decade.

I voted against the McConnell-Ryan tax plan because it was wrong for Hoosiers, plain and simple.

///

Whew, this turned into a long email! Thanks for reading it all the way through, Peter. **If you have a comment on anything I wrote above -- or if there 's anything else you'd like me to know -- just hit reply and shoot me an email.**

I appreciate all of your support,

In Case You Missed It!

Governor Phil Scott Presents Comprehensive 5-Year Plan to Improve Education Quality and Affordability While Achieving Tax Rate Stabilization

On Tuesday Governor Phil Scott presented a five-year plan (http://governor.vermont.gov/sites/scott/files/documents/5%20Year%20Education%20Plan%205-1-18.pdf) that prevents a $58 million tax hike, ensures property rates don’t increase for 5-years, and generates nearly $300 million in savings that can be used to lower tax rates, expand educational opportunities like early care and learning, or invest in technical education. The goal is to see the quality of education improve, which is why the Governor has consistently proposed structural reforms that provide tax relief and generate significant savings to be reinvested in kids, not old, inefficient systems.

Further, this plan reverses recurring structural deficits in the Education Fund. For years, Vermonters and Vermont’s economy have struggled under nearly non-stop increases in state education property taxes, and if we do nothing, we face a $58 million tax increase that the Governor will not accept, because Vermonters -- and Vermont’s economy -- cannot afford it.

“We all share a goal to provide our kids with the best education possible and have committed tremendous resources to K-12 education. The challenge lies in consistently declining enrollment and an old and inefficient infrastructure, which is diverting investments away from kids and causing taxes to increase at unsustainable rates,” said Governor Scott. “Vermont has great schools and teachers and delivers quality education to our students. But we can do better, and this plan will help us achieve our goals of a more affordable and higher quality education system.”

The Governor’s plan is kick-started by eliminating the FY19 deficit. The ongoing savings in the Governor’s plan will come from:
* Aligning staffing levels through retirements and other natural attrition (not refilling about 900 of the 5,000 retirement vacancies projected in the next 5 years) to bring staffing ratios up to about 5 students to each staff member(still the lowest in the country);
* Implementing a statewide healthcare benefit the Governor proposed last year;
* Improving the management of special education (does not decrease support for special education);
* Changes to income sensitivity; and
* Changes to excess spending thresholds set for high spending school districts.

For more details on the Governor’s plan, clickhere (http://governor.vermont.gov/sites/scott/files/documents/5%20Year%20Education%20Plan%205-1-18.pdf) .

body:
Hi Peter,

We're so excited to share that Swing Left, an organization dedicated to flipping red seats blue, has added Virginia's 7th Congressional District to their target list.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Only 11 Syrian refugees since January.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The Trump administration is turning its back on refugees. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd794b8/2f5ea88d/71fcbb0d/2cbcdd44/631745532/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&utm_campaign=refugee-letter&utm_medium=ema") Friend, This is nothing short of shameful. Inhumane. Wrong -- plain and simple. Around the world, people are struggling for survival in countries where violence, war, persecution, and poverty deprive them and their children of any chance for a secure future. But the Trump administration is slamming the door shut. The welcoming torch of Lady Liberty is being snuffed out. And President Trump is proving yet again: He's all talk, no action. Last month, I wrote a letter to the State Department demanding that they reverse their new policies and accept more refugees in light of the huge number of folks fleeing violence. Yet they haven't acted -- even in the face of further violence from the Syrian government. [**I need your help to make this happen. Urge the Trump administration to immediately review and reverse the policies restricting the ability of qualified refugees to be admitted into the United States. Add your name here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd794b8/2f5ea88d/71fcbb0d/2cbcdd44/631745532/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&utm_campaign=refugee-letter&utm_medium=ema") The world is experiencing the worst refugee crisis since WWII -- 22 million people have been displaced. Yet the Trump administration is refusing to admit thousands of qualified refugees fleeing unspeakable horrors in Syria and other regions affected by violence. This isn't who we are as a nation. Instead of providing a lifeline as we have done throughout our history, we are turning our back. We must renew our American commitment to supporting those in great need during a time of crisis. Last year, the president announced the United States would cap the number of refugees allowed into the country at 45,000 -- a historic low. But right now, approximately 21,000 refugees will be admitted this year, less than half of the president's own commitment. And it's not because people aren't in need: Despite a brutal civil war and inhumane chemical attacks by the Syrian government, **only 11 Syrians have been admitted to the United States since January.** _That's unconscionable -- and unacceptable._ It's time for the Trump administration to do more. This is, after all, about humanity. [**We're stronger when we demand action together. Help me out -- add your name to demand the Trump administration lift its harsh policies restricting qualified refugees from coming to the United States. Sign on here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd794b8/2f5ea88d/71fcbb0d/2cbcdd44/631745532/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&utm_campaign=refugee-letter&utm_medium=ema") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd794b8/2f5ea88d/71fcbb0d/2cbcdd44/631745532/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&source=ema_2018-05-03_refugee-letter&utm_campaign=refugee-letter&utm_medium=ema")

| Everyone -- Rudy Giuliani just admitted that Donald Trump personally reimbursed his "fixer" Michael Cohen for $130,000 that Cohen spent to silence Stormy Daniels.

**That could be a major campaign finance violation, and Trump is FURIOUS word is out.**

**When Trump goes off the rails, he lashes out at anyone and everyone in his way. That means we 're one step closer to an already impending constitutional crisis.**

name: Ben Jealous
subject: You're Invited: Debt-Free College Rally with Bernie Sanders
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
My family was only able to make it out of public housing in Baltimore due to the investments Maryland used to make in public higher education. However, as the war on drugs pushed more and more funding toward mass incarceration, our public colleges and universities faced funding shortfalls year after year.
We would love for you to join us at this event on May 7th with my good friend Senator Bernie Sanders to discuss my plan for debt-free college which I'll be releasing on Monday. **[RSVP here to let us know you're coming.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7f6/7ab0e2c4/7fbe6a66/349a4fc8/1131407701/VEsH/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/debt-free-college-sanders?source=EMD03058A") **
Let's end this era of student debt and pay for it by ending the era of mass incarceration.
